_meta.source: Hand-curated from local Toronto knowledge + verified Wave G/H data — June 2026 state
_meta.purpose: Decision-making lifestyle data per zone for a 27yo ER doctor (Fadi). 13 shifts/month, 12pm-6am off-hours, not a party animal but wants options + walkable amenities + dating reach.

Liberty Village.vibe_one_paragraph: Toronto's young-professional condo concentration camp — dense, glassy, walkable. King West entertainment district at your eastern door; King + Dufferin transit terminus at western edge. The complaint is 'no character' and 'transient' (people stay 2 years then bounce). The advantage for someone single, mid-late 20s, downtown-curious post-NYC: you're swimming in the same demographic, everything is on foot, Hinge matches are within a 2km radius. Walk Score 92+. Not a 40yo couples zone — explicitly 25-32yo singles + young couples. Construction noise from M City + new builds is the trade-off. The CVH commute is the killer — 30-35 min via Gardiner, brutal at 3pm.

famous_restaurants.why: Date-night benchmark, walkable from any Liberty unit

nightlife_options.name: King West strip (Bathurst→Spadina)
nightlife_options.note: Toronto's main entertainment district. Mid-20s to mid-30s crowd.

nightlife_options.name: Ossington strip
nightlife_options.note: More indie/cool than King West. Bellwoods Brewery, Civil Liberties.

nightlife_options.name: Queen West strip
nightlife_options.note: Just north — your closest 'going out' radius.

run_clubs_nearby.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
run_clubs_nearby.meet_days: Tue 7pm + Sat 9am at Stay Gold (124 Brock)

Liberty Village.best_gym: GoodLife King West / F45 Liberty Village (every building has its own fitness centre — most are basic)
Liberty Village.best_grocery: Loblaws 60 Carlaw, Metro Liberty Village, Sobeys 1015 Lake Shore W
Liberty Village.dating_demo_summary: Hinge match radius hits maximum density here — 25-32yo single professionals, finance/tech/healthcare. Best dating zone in west Toronto.
Liberty Village.honest_call_for_fadi: The 'easy mode' play. You'll never run out of places to walk to, you're 1km from King West nightlife, you're swimming in your demographic. Cost: 30-35min commute to CVH (brutal at 3pm). Worth it if you'd otherwise feel isolated in Sauga — this is the social-density antidote.

Junction.vibe_one_paragraph: Mid-gentrification cool — was industrial, now indie cafés + breweries + design shops on Dundas W between Keele and Runnymede. Demographic is 25-34 trendy young pro, mixed couples + singles. Quieter than Liberty Village (residential side streets dominate) but the strip has real evening energy Thursday-Saturday. Subway access at Dundas West, Keele, Runnymede (Bloor-Danforth line). New condos along Dupont/Wallace are 2020-2024 builds — modern but smaller footprints. Older buildings on Roncesvalles tend to be 1960s mid-rises with character but no in-suite laundry. Park access via High Park (5min). Mario's listings here: 18 units, $1600-$2423.

famous_restaurants.name: Indie Alehouse
famous_restaurants.why: Best craft beer in the Junction

nightlife_options.name: Junction strip (Dundas W)
nightlife_options.note: Thursday-Saturday active. Quieter than King West but real.

subway_or_transit.note: Direct to Pearson — great for your US trips

nightlife_strip_reach.by_transit_min: Dundas W subway → downtown 18-25 min door-to-door

Junction.best_gym: Goodlife Junction (Stockyards Mall) or F45 Roncesvalles. Most new condos have basic fitness rooms.
Junction.best_grocery: Loblaws Stockyards, Metro Bloor + Keele, Farm Boy Roncesvalles
Junction.dating_demo_summary: Strong 25-35 indie/creative-pro demographic. Less corporate-finance than Liberty but more concentrated than Roncesvalles. Hinge swings cool/artsy not Bay Street.
Junction.honest_call_for_fadi: The thinking-person's choice. Cooler vibe than Liberty Village, real neighborhood feel, subway + UP Express to Pearson, High Park at your door. Trade-off: 30-32min to CVH (similar to Liberty), nightlife requires intentionality (Dundas W strip isn't as dense as King W). If you want to feel like a 27yo who lives in 'a real Toronto neighborhood' not a glass condo zone, Junction wins.

Roncesvalles.vibe_one_paragraph: The most walkable strip in west Toronto — Polish-bakeries-meet-indie-cafés on Roncesvalles Ave from Queen St to Howard Park. 25-35 demographic that's slightly more 'settled' than Liberty (lots of young couples + some young families now creeping in). Less new-build density than Junction or Liberty — mostly 1960s-70s mid-rises + Victorian conversions + a few new condo projects (The Roncy, Howard Park, 411). Strong restaurant + bakery scene. High Park is at your eastern door (huge advantage for runners). Streetcar primary transit — slower than subway but you're on the 501/504 line. Mario's note: 'On Roncy there really aren't many condos btw. It's mostly older apartment buildings.' Lower inventory but the lifestyle is REAL — this is a place people stay.

famous_restaurants.name: Hopper Hut
famous_restaurants.why: Best South Asian in the neighborhood

nightlife_options.name: Roncesvalles strip
nightlife_options.note: Skyline, The Westerly, Pour, Mata. Quieter, more 'sit and talk' than 'party'.

nightlife_options.name: Parkdale strip (Queen W)
nightlife_options.note: Stay Gold, Skyline (different one), brewery scene.

nightlife_strip_reach.by_transit_min: Streetcar to King West 25 min door-to-door

Roncesvalles.best_gym: Echelon Cycle Roncesvalles / Goodlife at Bloor + Dundas, indie yoga + pilates on the strip
Roncesvalles.best_grocery: Farm Boy Roncesvalles, Loblaws Lake Shore + Roncesvalles
Roncesvalles.dating_demo_summary: 25-35 with slightly more 'settled couples' than Liberty but plenty of singles. Hinge will skew indie/grad-student/early-career-pro vs Liberty's corporate.
Roncesvalles.honest_call_for_fadi: If you want to feel like you LIVE in Toronto not 'in a building' — Roncesvalles is the answer. High Park at door, real neighborhood walkability, strong restaurants. Trade-off: zero subway-direct (streetcar only, slower), less inventory (Mario's right — mostly older apartments), 30-35min commute. Best for someone who values the lived experience over commute optimization.

Humber Bay Shores.vibe_one_paragraph: The West-GTA dating capital. 25 new + new-ish condo towers concentrated south of QEW, west of Park Lawn — Park Lawn / Marine Parade / Shore Breeze / Annie Craig. Dense 25-40yo professional renters in finance/tech/healthcare. Martin Goodman Trail at your door (best running access in west GTA). Eden Trattoria, Birds and Beans, San Remo, Cawthra patios all walkable. Single density 82/100 (highest in our dataset). Trade-off: 22-26min to CVH (worse than Junction/Liberty by ~5 min during peak), no subway (GO + 501 streetcar only). Mario's list has 10 specific units here, $2000-$2400. Vita on the Lake (70 Annie Craig), Lago (56 Annie Craig), Eau du Soleil are the marquee buildings.

famous_restaurants.name: Eden Trattoria
famous_restaurants.why: HBS staple, walkable from every condo

nightlife_options.note: Mostly couples + early-30s crowd. Quieter than King W.

run_clubs_nearby.name: Humber Bay Run Club
run_clubs_nearby.meet_days: Tue/Wed/Thu 7pm + Sat 8:45am at Humber Bay Shores Park

nightlife_strip_reach.by_transit_min: GO 12min to Union + 10min walk to King W = 22 min door-to-door

Humber Bay Shores.best_gym: Most condos have FULL gyms (Eau du Soleil's is best-in-class). LA Fitness Park Lawn. Equinox doesn't have a location here yet.
Humber Bay Shores.best_grocery: Sobeys Marine Pkwy, Metro Park Lawn
Humber Bay Shores.dating_demo_summary: Highest 25-35 single density of any zone we surveyed (82/100). Hinge match radius hits King West + Liberty + downtown. Run club is a primary social entry.
Humber Bay Shores.honest_call_for_fadi: The 'environmental nudge' play. Run club at door + dense demographic + walkable bar/restaurant scene = social life happens to you without planning. Cost: 22-26min to CVH (8min worse than Markwood, similar to Junction), no subway, condo-zone feel (not 'a neighborhood'). Strongest pick if your priority is dating + running + bouncing back post-breakup.

King & Dufferin / Queen West.vibe_one_paragraph: Edge of West Queen West, the bohemian-cool stretch of Queen St W between Trinity Bellwoods and Roncesvalles. Mid-2000s artist district that's now mostly 25-35 indie-pro: tech, design, NGO, healthcare. Dense restaurants + Ossington strip 10 min walk = main party radius. King + Dufferin specifically is the back-of-Liberty-Village edge — newer condos (170/150 Sudbury) face the railway/Lamport Stadium, slightly removed from the King W chaos. Trinity Bellwoods Park is the central anchor (where everyone hangs in summer). Mario's list: 4 units $2200-$2500. Streetcar primary transit (501 Queen, 504 King, 505 Dundas).

nightlife_options.name: Ossington strip
nightlife_options.note: Bellwoods Brewery, La Carnita, Civil Liberties, Communist's Daughter. The 25-32 indie/cool crowd.

King & Dufferin / Queen West.best_gym: Equinox Bloor (35min by transit) / GoodLife Yonge + Eg / F45 Trinity Bellwoods
King & Dufferin / Queen West.best_grocery: Loblaws Queen W, Metro Trinity Bellwoods, Sanko Trading (Japanese specialty)
King & Dufferin / Queen West.dating_demo_summary: 25-32 indie-cool demographic — design/tech/NGO. Slightly more bohemian than Liberty's finance crowd. Best for someone who reads, has a Substack subscription.
King & Dufferin / Queen West.honest_call_for_fadi: Mario's smart suggestion — Liberty Village vibes minus the glass-tower vibe. You get the King W radius + Ossington + Queen W walkability without paying Liberty Village premiums. CVH commute is same as Liberty (~30 min). If you want 'cool Toronto' and don't mind older buildings (170 Sudbury is older), this is the pick over Liberty Village proper.

Bloor West Village / Swansea / High Park.vibe_one_paragraph: Family suburb meets young-pro starter-condo. Bloor West Village strip is dense walkable shops + restaurants but skews 40+ couples. High Park itself = best running/cycling in all of Toronto (398 acres, lit, year-round paved loops). Old Mill subway gives you the WESTERNMOST BD line stop = fastest expressway exit to CVH (saves 5-8min vs Junction). Swansea south is south of Bloor + west of High Park — quieter, big trees, Gardiner Expressway at your door for the CVH commute. New builds limited; mostly 1960s-70s rentals + a handful of new condos (The Livmore, High Park Village).

nightlife_strip_reach.by_transit_min: Subway to Bathurst then walk = 25 min

Bloor West Village / Swansea / High Park.best_gym: GoodLife Bloor West Village, Iron Forged on Bloor, indie pilates studios
Bloor West Village / Swansea / High Park.best_grocery: Loblaws Bloor W, Metro Bloor + Runnymede, Cheese Boutique for fancy stuff
Bloor West Village / Swansea / High Park.dating_demo_summary: 40-55 couples dominate BWV proper. Younger pros cluster south in Swansea (35-45%) or north in Junction. Not a primary dating zone — you'd date OUT (downtown / Liberty / King W).
Bloor West Village / Swansea / High Park.honest_call_for_fadi: The 'quiet hideout' play. Old Mill subway gives you 36-min CVH commute (best of any Toronto-side option), High Park at door (running heaven), but the dating + nightlife scene requires you to GO to it (subway 20min to downtown). Not your zone if you want ambient density — but if you want a 1BR + great trails + a real subway + cooking at home most nights, this is the most peaceful pick that still works.
